step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("peg-shaped incisors congenital syphilis rickets anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia supernumerary teeth")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the reviewed literature, peg-shaped incisors that taper lingually are characteristic of congenital syphilis and rickets, among others. While anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia can indeed feature dental anomalies, it does not typically present with peg-shaped incisors unless combined with other factors such as nutritional deficiencies. Conversely, supernumerary teeth are unrelated to the shape of natural incisors except in very special circumstances where additional teeth overlap normal ones anatomically. Given that the question asks about the exception - i.e., what does NOT fit commonly alongside those typical deformities - the clear outlier here is D: Supernumerary teeth. Thus I reaffirm agreement with the prior conclusion.
